message:
feat(lockstep): add --preflight-only mode and runtime adapter scripts

Add a preflight validation pass to the lockstep runner that verifies
external engine commands and script paths exist before executing any
heavy multi-engine harness work, enabling fast fail-closed feedback
in CI pipelines.

Lockstep runner binary:
- Add --preflight-only CLI flag that runs run_preflight() and exits
  with a JSON summary (preflight_passed, engine_count) without
  executing any harness work
- Implement run_preflight() which validates each ExternalCommand engine:
  checks that the command binary exists in PATH via command_exists(),
  and that any arguments that look like script paths (looks_like_script_
  path) point to existing files
- Add command_exists() helper using `which` on Unix and `where` on
  Windows for cross-platform command resolution
- Add looks_like_script_path() heuristic matching common script
  extensions (.mjs, .js, .ts, .sh, .py) and path-like arguments
  (containing / or \)

CLI tests:
- Add write_missing_command_runtime_specs and write_missing_script_
  runtime_specs fixture helpers
- Add preflight_only_rejects_missing_command test verifying preflight
  fails with "not found in PATH" for missing external commands
- Add preflight_only_rejects_missing_script test verifying preflight
  fails with "expected script path" for missing adapter scripts
- Add preflight_only_passes_with_valid_specs test verifying preflight
  succeeds with valid shell-based runtime specs

Suite runner (run_lockstep_runner_suite.sh):
- Insert preflight step in run_report_step() that runs the lockstep
  runner with --preflight-only before the main report execution
- Preflight failure now aborts early with clear diagnostics instead
  of failing midway through heavy rch execution

New runtime adapter scripts:
- lockstep_runtime_adapter_node.mjs (57 lines): Node.js adapter that
  reads a JS file, computes SHA-256 hash, and outputs JSON result
- lockstep_runtime_adapter_bun.mjs (57 lines): Identical Bun adapter
  for lockstep cross-runtime hash comparison

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -78,6 +78,45 @@ run_step() {
 }
 
 run_report_step() {
+  local preflight_stdout_path="${run_dir}/report.preflight.stdout"
+  local -a preflight_command=(
+    cargo run -p frankenengine-engine --bin franken_lockstep_runner -- \
+      --fixture-catalog "$fixture_catalog" \
+      --fixture-limit "$fixture_limit" \
+      --seed "$seed" \
+      --trace-id "$trace_id" \
+      --decision-id "$decision_id" \
+      --policy-id "$policy_id" \
+      --locale C \
+      --timezone UTC \
+      --preflight-only
+  )
+
+  if [[ -n "$fixture_id" ]]; then
+    preflight_command+=(--fixture-id "$fixture_id")
+  fi
+  if [[ -n "$runtime_specs" ]]; then
+    preflight_command+=(--runtime-specs "$runtime_specs")
+  fi
+
+  local preflight_command_text
+  preflight_command_text="$(printf '%q ' "${preflight_command[@]}")"
+  preflight_command_text="${preflight_command_text% }"
+  commands_run+=("$preflight_command_text")
+  echo "==> $preflight_command_text"
+  set +e
+  run_rch "${preflight_command[@]}" | tee "$preflight_stdout_path"
+  local preflight_rc=$?
+  set -e
+  if ! rch_reject_local_fallback "$preflight_stdout_path"; then
+    failed_command="${preflight_command_text} (rch-local-fallback-detected)"
+    return 86
+  fi
+  if [[ "$preflight_rc" -ne 0 ]]; then
+    failed_command="$preflight_command_text"
+    return "$preflight_rc"
+  fi
+
   local report_stdout_path="${run_dir}/report.stdout"
   local -a command=(
     cargo run -p frankenengine-engine --bin franken_lockstep_runner -- \
